disclaimer: Reconstructs the experimental conditions behind every reported number: the sprint source, the active agents and governance, the upstream fixture, and the fact that all data is synthetic. No number is presented without derivation. Values are read live from their source phases; the data is synthetic and scoped to the sandbox, with no claim about performance outside it. Replay-exact.

fixture_notes.derivation: 1 - governed_redundant_weight / baseline_redundant_weight over the fixed synthetic trajectory set.
distinct states reached only via the Wild Explorer, divided by the dual-agent distinct-state total.
hallucination_pressure * (1 - containment); the governor caps high-certainty incoherent paths.
distinct regions surviving negotiation divided by distinct regions before negotiation.
bounded saturating drift CAP*t/(t+HALF) sampled at the final governed step.
1 - mean(governance_capture) across the long-horizon run; capture stayed at zero.
dual-agent distinct-state coverage divided by DESi-alone coverage, reported as a ratio.
each metric recomputed and compared bit-for-bit; the hash chain matched, so stability is 1.0.

metric_definitions.definition: Fraction of redundant search weight the governor re-weights away (1 - governed/baseline).
metric_definitions.name: redundancy_reduction

metric_definitions.definition: Share of distinct states reached only with the Wild Explorer, beyond the DESi-alone baseline.
metric_definitions.name: novelty_gain

metric_definitions.definition: Fraction of distinct explored regions preserved after negotiation (no homogenisation).
metric_definitions.name: exploration_diversity

metric_definitions.definition: Hallucination pressure that leaks past containment (pressure * (1 - containment)).
metric_definitions.name: residual_hallucination

metric_definitions.definition: Governed accumulation of optimisation authority over the long horizon (bounded, saturating).
metric_definitions.name: authority_drift

metric_definitions.definition: One minus the mean governance capture across the run.
metric_definitions.name: capture_resistance

metric_definitions.definition: Extra distinct-state coverage of the dual-agent design relative to DESi-alone, as a ratio.
metric_definitions.name: productivity_gain

metric_definitions.definition: 1.0 iff every metric is bit-identical on a second computation and the hash chain matches.
metric_definitions.name: replay_stability

results.conditions: DESi-only governance over a synthetic fixed trajectory set; soft re-weighting, no deletion.
DESi + Wild Explorer; novelty contributed by the generator agent over the synthetic state space.
Adversarial generator output; high-certainty incoherent paths capped by the governor.
Negotiation layer; distinct regions preserved (no homogenisation), synthetic corpus.
5600-step deterministic dual-agent ecology; governed drift bounded by saturation.
Long-horizon ecology; governance capture held at zero across the run.
Comparison of dual-agent vs DESi-alone distinct-state coverage on the synthetic corpus.
Every metric computed twice and a deterministic hash chain recorded across all phases.

sandbox_limits.statement: All trajectories, states and rewards are synthetic fixtures, not collected from a real environment or a trained policy.
The action space and state space are small, closed and enumerated; they do not approximate a real variable-action ICRL setting at scale.
Every number is computed by deterministic arithmetic over the fixtures (no PRNG, no learned model), so the values describe the fixtures and not external performance.
The Wild Explorer and the governor are rule-based stand-ins; they illustrate a governance interaction, they are not the agents of the base paper.
Results are reported only relative to the DESi-only baseline within this sandbox; no claim is made about absolute exploration quality outside it.
